title: Board Permissions also locking activity log

posts.author: FredW (Frederik Wongel)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone,

we recently restricted viewing permissions on some of our boards, so that freelancers we work together with are only able to view elements they are assigned to.

However, I noticed, that this change also prevents them from viewing the activity logs of elements, as well as everyone else who isn’t a board-owner.

Since we also have “regular” employees working with these boards, who are supposed to be able to look at the activity logs this is really inconvenient.
Is there a way to make the activity log available to people without making them board-owners?

Kind regards

Fred

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @FredW,

I hear where you’re coming from. At this stage, I am afraid as long as those permissions are set, there will always be a restriction on the item activity log. We have received feedback about this in the past, so I will be sure to add your comments. Thank you in advance for your understanding

posts.author: FredW (Frederik Wongel)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @BiancaT ,

thanks for the response.
Good to know this is somethng you are aware of, hopefully some more options for permission-settings will be implemented in the future.
It would be generally very useful to be able to customize board-permissions in more detail, not just regarding the activity log

posts.author: Iryna_Belaya (Iryna Belaya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @FredW ,
If you’re open to the apps, Board Email Reports can serve as a workaround.
It aggregates the changes in the activity log by the column you choose and over the period you choose in the automation rule, and sends the xls-report to any people you mention in the rule (no guest access needed!).
Report types

On a board/project level:

  1. New and updated tasks
  2. Status updates
  3. Budget, Costs, Revenue changes
  4. Timeline and deadline overdue
  5. Time Tracking changes

On an item/task level:

  • Updates by a specific employee
  • Updates mentioning a specific employee
  • Updates containing attachments
  • Status updates
  • Last updates

An example:

image1202×578 63.7 KB

The email you get will contain the xls-report and roundup of changes:

image464×425 28.5 KB

I hope you will find it helpful.
